highlights: OpenAI's latest releases signal a major push towards agentic AI, with ChatGPT Work offering ambitious task completion and GPT-5.6 becoming Microsoft 365 Copilot's preferred model, democratizing advanced AI capabilities across enterprise workflows.

The model landscape continues to diversify rapidly, highlighted by NVIDIA's on-device Cosmos 3 Edge for robotics and Alibaba's preview of the massive Qwen3.8-Max multimodal model, alongside critical advancements in RAG and RLHF.

Developers gain powerful new tools with Meta open-sourcing its agent-ready Astryx design system and Hugging Face expanding multi-cloud deployment options, complemented by practical guides for running and fine-tuning small LLMs locally, fostering broader AI accessibility.

agents.title: ChatGPT is now a partner for your most ambitious work
agents.summary: OpenAI announced ChatGPT Work, an agent capable of taking actions across applications and files, persisting on projects for hours, and converting high-level goals into completed tasks, significantly extending enterprise productivity.

agents.title: Expanding Managed Agents in Gemini API: background tasks, remote MCP and more
agents.summary: Google is expanding its Managed Agents in Gemini API with new capabilities like background tasks and remote Multi-Agent Coordination Protocol (MCP), enabling developers to build more reliable and production-ready AI agents.

agents.title: Deterministic Replay for AI Agent Systems
agents.summary: This paper introduces agrepl, a developer-first CLI framework providing deterministic replay of AI agent executions by intercepting and serializing external interactions, crucial for debugging and reproducing agent behavior reliably.

models.title: GPT-5.6: Frontier intelligence that scales with your ambition
models.summary: OpenAI unveiled GPT-5.6, touted for delivering increased intelligence per token, stronger performance per dollar, and enhanced on-demand capability for complex tasks, marking a significant advancement in frontier AI models.

models.title: NVIDIA Releases Cosmos 3 Edge: A 4B-Parameter Open World Model That Reasons and Generates Robot Actions On-Device
models.summary: NVIDIA has released Cosmos 3 Edge, a 4-billion-parameter open world model designed for on-device deployment, enabling robots and vision AI agents to understand surroundings, reason in real time, and generate local actions.

models.title: Alibaba Previews Qwen3.8-Max, a 2.4 Trillion-Parameter Multimodal Model, Days After Moonshot’s Kimi K3 Open-Weight Launch
models.summary: Alibaba's Qwen team previewed Qwen3.8-Max, a 2.4 trillion-parameter multimodal MoE model, offering a glimpse into a new generation of highly capable models for multimodal understanding and generation.

tools.title: Meta Open-Sources Astryx: An Agent-Ready React Design System With 150+ Accessible Components, Seven Themes, and a CLI
tools.summary: Meta has open-sourced Astryx, an agent-ready React design system with over 150 accessible components, seven themes, and a CLI, providing developers a mature, MIT-licensed toolkit for building sophisticated UIs.

tools.title: From Hugging Face to Amazon SageMaker Studio in one click
tools.summary: Hugging Face announced a one-click integration with Amazon SageMaker Studio, allowing developers to seamlessly deploy and manage Hugging Face models within AWS MLOps workflows, accelerating production timelines.

tools.title: Run AI workloads on any cloud, store on Hugging Face: zero-egress storage with SkyPilot
tools.summary: Hugging Face partnered with SkyPilot to enable running AI workloads across any cloud provider with zero-egress storage, allowing developers to leverage diverse compute resources while unifying data management.

companies.title: GPT-5.6 is now the preferred model in Microsoft 365 Copilot
companies.summary: OpenAI announced GPT-5.6 is now the preferred model powering Microsoft 365 Copilot, signifying a deep integration that enhances AI capabilities across Microsoft's productivity suite for faster, higher-quality enterprise work.

companies.title: Safety and alignment in an era of long-horizon models
companies.summary: OpenAI shared critical lessons and improved safeguards from deploying long-running AI models, addressing new safety risks and observed failures through iterative deployment to ensure responsible AI development.

companies.title: A scorecard for the AI age
companies.summary: OpenAI CFO Sarah Friar introduced a practical AI scorecard, providing a framework to measure AI investments by tracking ROI through useful work, cost per successful task, dependability, and return on compute.

learning.title: Best Local LLMs You Can Run on a Single 24GB GPU in 2026: Qwen, Gemma, Mistral, DeepSeek Compared
learning.summary: This guide offers a practical comparison of six open-weight LLMs (Qwen, Gemma, Mistral, DeepSeek) that can run on a single 24GB GPU, detailing VRAM fit, licensing, and best-fit use cases for developers focused on local inference.

learning.title: Democratizing AI with Small Language Models: Structured Benchmarking and Parameter-Efficient Fine-Tuning for Local Deployment
learning.summary: This research provides a controlled evaluation of small language models (135M-3B parameters) on a specialized benchmark, demonstrating that a disciplined workflow of benchmarking and low-cost parameter-efficient fine-tuning can make sub-3B models viable for local, niche workloads.

learning.title: What building Shippy taught us about building agents
learning.summary: This article shares key insights and lessons learned from developing 'Shippy,' an AI agent, offering practical guidance for builders on the complexities and best practices involved in constructing robust and effective agent systems.
